<!-- GENERATED by scripts/sync_agent_assets.py from agents/skills/foam-setup/SKILL.md — edit the source and re-run the script; do not edit this copy. -->

# Foam-Agent setup and health check

The `foamagent` MCP server runs in Docker and needs no API key. This skill
gets it from "nothing works" to "healthy" with the minimum number of steps.
Work through the checks IN ORDER — each assumes the previous ones passed.
(No Foam-Agent clone on this machine? Skip to
[the global install path](#global-install-drive-foam-agent-from-your-own-project).)

**When to run this — conditionally, not ritually:** on first setup of a
fresh clone or machine, after an update, or when the foamagent tools stop
responding mid-session. A healthy setup needs no per-session preflight —
if the tools answer, skip straight to work.

Shortcut: the doctor runs checks 2–5 below deterministically (read-only)
and prints exact fix commands — run it first to see where things stand,
then work the failing step here. In a clone that is
`python scripts/doctor.py`; on a global install the doctor travels with
this skill: `python <this-skill's-directory>/references/doctor.py`.

## 1. Quick health check

Call the `get_case_stats` MCP tool. If it returns lists of domains,
categories and ~50 solvers, the server is healthy — stop here.

If the tool isn't available or the connection is refused, continue below.
(No MCP tools wired in your session? The endpoint is
`http://localhost:7860/mcp` — you can drive it from Python with the
`fastmcp` client; see `tests/test_lid_driven_cavity_mcp.py` for the pattern.)

## 2. Is Docker available?

```powershell
# Windows: docker is often not on PATH in fresh shells
$env:PATH = "$env:ProgramFiles\Docker\Docker\resources\bin;$env:PATH"
docker version --format "{{.Server.Version}}"
```

No Docker daemon → ask the user to start Docker Desktop (or install Docker).
Do not try to install Docker yourself.

## 3. Does the image exist?

```powershell
docker images foamagent --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.Size}}"
```

If missing, prefer pulling the prebuilt image from GHCR (minutes, no LFS
needed — the FAISS indices are baked in) and tagging it with the local name
the rest of this skill uses:

```powershell
docker pull ghcr.io/kasperhonore/foamagent:latest
docker tag ghcr.io/kasperhonore/foamagent:latest foamagent:latest
```

Only if the pull fails (offline, GHCR unavailable) build from source. FIRST
verify git-lfs content is real (fresh clones often have pointer files
instead of the FAISS indices):

```powershell
# index.faiss files must be MB-sized; ~130-byte files are LFS pointers
Get-ChildItem database/faiss -Recurse -Filter *.faiss | Select-Object Length, FullName
git lfs pull   # if they are pointers
```

Then build — **warn the user this takes ~10 minutes and ~10 GB on first
build** (mostly the OpenFOAM base image). Run it in the background:

```powershell
docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t foamagent:latest .
```

## 4. Is the container running?

```powershell
docker ps -a --filter name=foamagent-mcp --format "{{.Names}} {{.Status}}"
```

- Exists but `Exited` → `docker start foamagent-mcp`
- Doesn't exist → create it:

```powershell
docker run -d --name foamagent-mcp --restart unless-stopped -p 7860:7860 `
  -v "${PWD}/runs:/home/openfoam/Foam-Agent/runs" `
  foamagent:latest python -m src.mcp.fastmcp_server --transport http --host 0.0.0.0 --port 7860
```

(Run from the repo root — the `-v` mount keeps simulation results in the
clone's `runs/` so they survive container recreation. On a global install
there is no clone: mount the central runs directory instead,
`-v "$HOME/foamagent/runs:/home/openfoam/Foam-Agent/runs"` — see the global
install section below. The image does not pip-install the package, so use
`python -m src.mcp.fastmcp_server`, not `foamagent-mcp`.)

Wait ~15 s, then confirm startup:

```powershell
# The $( ) wrapper matters on Windows PowerShell 5.1: a bare `2>&1 |` wraps
# docker's stderr lines in red NativeCommandError records even on success.
# Keep the 2>&1 itself — Uvicorn prints its banner to stderr.
$(docker logs foamagent-mcp 2>&1) | Select-String "Uvicorn running"
```

No "Uvicorn running" → read the full `docker logs foamagent-mcp` and
diagnose. Common: port 7860 already taken (another container or process —
`docker ps` / change the published port AND the MCP configs), or the
entrypoint failing to source OpenFOAM.

## 5. Verify end to end

Re-run the health check (step 1). Then be aware of two first-use behaviors:

- **First retrieval call** (`find_similar_case` / `search_tutorials`)
  downloads the ~1.2 GB Qwen embedding model inside the container and loads
  4 FAISS indices — expect several minutes of silence. It is NOT hung.
  Warm it proactively with a throwaway call, e.g.
  `search_tutorials(index="openfoam_tutorials_details", query="lid driven cavity")`.
- **Execution tools** (`run_case`, `run_openfoam_command`) need OpenFOAM,
  which the container sources automatically. If they fail with
  `WM_PROJECT_DIR is not set`, the entrypoint didn't run — recreate the
  container as in step 4.

## Global install: drive Foam-Agent from your own project

The supported path for agentic users who work from their own repositories
(spec #29 / issue #51): the product skills install into YOUR project with
standard tooling, every project's simulations land in one central runs
directory, and the doctor travels with the skills — no Foam-Agent clone
anywhere.

1. **Install the product skills** into the user's project (vercel-labs
   skills CLI):

   ```bash
   npx skills add KasperHonore/Foam-Agent
   ```

2. **Register the MCP server** with the user's harness:

   ```bash
   npx add-mcp http://localhost:7860/mcp
   ```

   Both are third-party CLIs; the forms above are the ones they document
   (`npx skills add <owner>/<repo>` — add `--list` to preview or
   `-a claude-code` to target one harness; `npx add-mcp <url>` detects the
   harnesses configured in the project). Verify with `--help` on first use —
   these tools are not covered by this repo's CI.

3. **Create the central runs directory and start the container.** All
   projects share one mount, `~/foamagent/runs` — the container path stays
   the same as on the clone path:

   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/foamagent/runs
   docker pull ghcr.io/kasperhonore/foamagent:latest
   docker tag ghcr.io/kasperhonore/foamagent:latest foamagent:latest
   docker run -d --name foamagent-mcp --restart unless-stopped -p 7860:7860 \
     -v "$HOME/foamagent/runs:/home/openfoam/Foam-Agent/runs" \
     foamagent:latest python -m src.mcp.fastmcp_server --transport http --host 0.0.0.0 --port 7860
   ```

   (PowerShell: `-v "$env:USERPROFILE\foamagent\runs:/home/openfoam/Foam-Agent/runs"`
   with backtick line continuations. In a clone you mount the clone's
   `runs/` instead — step 4 above.)

4. **Finish the residue conversationally** — the agent-driven leftovers no
   CLI covers: bring the container up if it is stopped (steps 2–4 above),
   warm the embedding model with a throwaway retrieval call (step 5 above),
   and register the subagent roles natively where the harness supports
   them — the installed skills bundle the role definitions under
   `references/subagents/` as inline fallbacks either way.

5. **Converge on doctor-green.** The doctor is bundled with this skill, so
   it runs from any directory with no clone:

   ```bash
   python <skills-install-path>/foam-setup/references/doctor.py
   ```

   (typically `.claude/skills/foam-setup/references/doctor.py` inside the
   user's project). Outside a clone it skips clone-only checks (git-lfs)
   with a note, expects the runs mount from the central directory, and
   compares the image's version stamp against the version stamped into the
   installed skills at bundle time. Green is the same install contract as
   the repo path. Central root somewhere else? Set `FOAMAGENT_HOME`
   (default `~/foamagent`) — the doctor and your `-v` mount must agree.

### One central ledger, many projects

With the central mount, the run ledger lives at the shared runs root —
`~/foamagent/runs/ledger.md` — one ledger covering every project, written
by the server exactly as on the clone path (zero server changes).
Per-project namespacing comes from nested case keys: resolve cases under a
per-project subdirectory (a case name like `bike-fairing/inlet-nozzle`, or
an explicit `case_dir` under `runs/<project>/`), and the ledger records the
case as its path relative to the runs root — `bike-fairing/inlet-nozzle` —
so listings stay unambiguous across projects. This is the shipped
`resolve_case_dir`/ledger behavior: nested paths keep their structure
(`src/ledger.py`), which is what namespaces projects sharing one runs
directory.

## Alternative: local install without Docker (Linux/macOS)

Only when the user explicitly wants it and has Foundation OpenFOAM v10
installed locally:

```bash
pip install -e .        # key-free core
foamagent-mcp           # stdio transport
```

Switch the committed MCP configs from the HTTP URL to
`"command": "foamagent-mcp"`. Retrieval works without OpenFOAM;
execution tools require `WM_PROJECT_DIR`.

## Troubleshooting summary

| Symptom | Cause → fix |
|---|---|
| `connection refused` on 7860 | Container not running → steps 2–4 |
| `docker: not recognized` (Windows) | PATH → step 2 prefix |
| Retrieval fails: index not loaded | LFS pointers → `git lfs pull`, rebuild image |
| First retrieval takes minutes | Embedding model download — wait, don't restart |
| `WM_PROJECT_DIR is not set` | Entrypoint bypassed → recreate container (step 4) |
| Port 7860 in use | Republish another port + update all committed MCP configs |
| `write_case_file`: `Permission denied` on an existing `runs/<case>` | Root-owned files left by a pre-non-root image → recreate the container from `:latest` (entrypoint repairs ownership at startup), or `docker exec -u root foamagent-mcp chown -R openfoam:openfoam /home/openfoam/Foam-Agent/runs` |
